title: Possible signs of ancient life on Mars are rich in complex carbon
description: An instrument on the Perseverance rover has identified large, complex carbon compounds alongside unusual patterns on the surface of rocks that resemble traces of microbial activity
author: Alex Wilkins

title: A note from the anniversary editor
description: Send us your memories of New Scientist for our 70th anniversary issue
author: Sam Wong

title: All known Homo naledi skeletons seem to be female
description: An analysis of tooth proteins suggests all 23 Homo naledi individuals found in the Rising Star cave in South Africa were female, which strengthens the case that they were placed there deliberately
author: Colin Barras

title: Some of the last Neanderthals were surprisingly genetically diverse
description: Genetic analysis of Neanderthals in north-western Europe reveals that this population was surprisingly genetically diverse, hinting that inbreeding didn’t lead to the species' demise
author: Chris Simms

title: Fluctuating oestrogen levels may alter how drugs enter women's brains
description: Oestrogen levels fluctuate throughout a woman's menstrual cycle, which may impact how efficiently a drug that targets the brain can reach its destination
author: Starre Vartan

title: Hidden black hole could explain mystery at the heart of our galaxy
description: The area surrounding our galaxy’s central supermassive black hole contains three strangely different populations of stars – but one hidden black hole could explain all of them
author: Leah Crane
